meta.subtitle: Park City Point 2 Point (~Sep 5) — first season with power (Assioma pedals since Jul 19)
meta.goal_leadville: Leadville Silver Rush 50 (Jul 12) CANCELED — wildfires. The base built for it rolls straight into the P2P build.
meta.goal_p2p: Finish strong & smart — durability is the weapon. Climbs ≤135 (cap 140) at 165–180 W, <5 punches/hr, fueled 60–80 g/hr on a timer from min 15.
principles: HR caps are LAW — the whole usable range is 27 bpm (118–145), so a few beats matter. Never chase anyone uphill. Including Jonathan. Especially Jonathan.
Pace climbs by POWER now: 165–180 W sustained (proven 58 min @ 180 on Pinecone 7/20). HR ceiling 140 is the backstop, not the target.
Punches ≥247 W are matches — <5/hr. Pre-shift before pitches, carry momentum, let speed sag.
Fuel on a 20-min timer from min 15, every ride ≥90 min — durability MASKS under-fueling until it's late (the 7/20 lesson).
Body composition is the biggest lever: each kg ≈ 5 m/h of VAM ≈ 10–12 min across P2P. Deficit on easy/rest days only; never cut in the final fortnight.
At 60, recovery gates everything: every 3rd week is a recovery week, sleep is a session, Mondays stay easy.
Most hours should feel EASY (HR <128) — raising LT1 (100 W lab) is the aerobic mission.

zones.use: Happens on climbs; don't live here
PACE BY POWER — HR can't resolve climb effort

zones.power: lab 190 W STALE → test wk of Jul 20 (expect 210–220)

weeks.label: Week of Jul 20 — Absorb the double block → FTP field test
weeks.focus: Recover from the Jul 19–20 back-to-back (2,800+ kJ). The week's one quality item is the FTP FIELD TEST once legs are fresh — the Dec lab 190 W is stale (40 min @ 190 at sub-threshold HR on 7/20 says ~210–220). Everything else stays P1/P2. Fix the Fenix FTP setting to 190 today; update after the test.

sessions.title: (ridden) 3.3 h / 3,448 ft
detail: Ridden before this plan was published — see the ride report on the board. Template Monday is rest; the recovery it displaced moves to Tue/Wed.

nutrition.notes: Long/hard day — NO deficit. Fuel the ride fully (60–80 g/hr on the timer), refeed after, carb-up the evening before the long day.

sessions.title: Easy spin 1:00
detail: Flush day after the double block. Walking counts. No strength.

nutrition.notes: Body-comp phase: modest deficit on easy/rest days ONLY. Protein every meal (95 kg → keep it high). On-bike protocol identical to Jonathan's Woodruff plan; daily macros are starting guidance pending Robert's own numbers.

sessions.title: 20-min FTP field test — or push to Friday if legs aren't fresh
sessions.targets: 20-min max steady effort on a consistent climb · FTP = 95% of 20-min power
detail: Warm up 20–25 min easy + 3×1 min builds. Then 20 min at the hardest pace you can hold STEADY — start conservative (~200 W), lift after minute 10 if it feels sustainable.
Use one continuous climb (Royal St / American Flag style grade) — steady gradient beats rollers for pacing.
This sets every training number: expect ~210–220 W. Update the Fenix and the board's bands after.
Not fresh today? Swap with Friday and ride 1.5 h base <128 instead.

nutrition.notes: Body-comp phase: modest deficit on easy/rest days ONLY. Protein every meal (95 kg → keep it high). On-bike protocol identical to Jonathan's Woodruff plan; daily macros are starting guidance pending Robert's own numbers.

sessions.title: Base 1.5 h
detail: Easy aerobic volume — the LT1-raising work. Bulk of the week lives here.

sessions.title: Bodyweight strength after the ride
detail: Step-ups 3×10/leg · chair squats 3×12 · calf raises 3×15 · planks 3×30 s. Nothing that leaves soreness for Saturday.

nutrition.notes: Body-comp phase: modest deficit on easy/rest days ONLY. Protein every meal (95 kg → keep it high). On-bike protocol identical to Jonathan's Woodruff plan; daily macros are starting guidance pending Robert's own numbers.

days.headline: Easy or rest (test day if Wednesday was skipped)

sessions.title: Easy 1:00 or rest
detail: Set up Saturday. If the FTP test hasn't happened yet, it happens today — fresh.

nutrition.notes: Rest day — the main weekly cut lives here. Never cut protein. If weight stalls on short sleep, fix sleep before cutting harder.

sessions.title: Long aerobic 3.5–4 h
sessions.targets: HR <133 all day · climbs ≤135 by feel · fuel on the 20-min timer
detail: Jonathan rides his 4:00 in Park City today — a joint long ride works perfectly; his cap is HR ≤138, yours ≤133, both genuinely easy.
Practice the race fueling exactly: 60–80 g/hr from min 15. Durability masks under-fueling — the timer, not feel.
Punch budget applies even easy: pre-shift before pitches, <5 surges ≥247 W per hour.

fueling.do: Start carb mix + sips — feed from min 15 on a 20-min timer, every ride, no exceptions
~30 g carb + electrolytes (target 60–80 g/hr · 500–800 mg sodium/hr · 20–24 oz fluid/hr)

sessions.title: Climb intervals 3×8–10 min @ HR 138–144
detail: On a steady climb. Start at 8-min reps; these lift LT1/LT2 over time.
HR ceiling is law — 144 means 144. Power will tell you if you're drifting hot once the FTP is set.

weeks.label: Week of Aug 31 — RACE: Park City Point 2 Point, Sat Sep 5
weeks.focus: Execution only. Carb-load Thu–Fri, openers light, sleep banked Thursday night (that's the night that matters). Race: climbs ≤135 (ceiling 140) at 165–180 W, <5 punches/hr, fuel from min 15, ride YOUR race — built to finish strong.

sessions.title: RACE: P2P
sessions.targets: Climbs ≤135 (cap 140) · 165–180 W sustained climbs · <5 punches/hr · fuel from min 15 · 500–800 mg sodium/hr
detail: 2–3 h pre: big carb breakfast (as rehearsed Aug 22). Easy 10–15 min spin before the start.
First half is discipline: let groups go on every climb. The field comes back to the diesel after hour six — that's YOUR race.
Feed alarm every 20 min, first feed at min 15, no skipped feeds ever — the 7/20 lesson: the engine outlasts unfueled legs.
Descents: coast, recover, eat. Finish strong — that's the goal, and it's built.
